input_dict.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.
input_dict.base_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Agent role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

Pipeline position: after summary1 and before second-hop retrieval. Inputs are the original question and the first-hop intermediate state. Functional responsibility: produce one focused retrieval query that is likely to recover the missing evidence needed by the downstream agents. Query quality is determined by evidence recovery, not by whether the query itself resembles or contains the final answer.

Native output contract: The module must consume `question` and `summary_1` and produce exactly one compact second-hop query field named `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include every distinct entity token visible in the inputs in the single terse query and place at most one neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the targeted relation or attribute (for example: "EntityA EntityB director").
- If a visible disambiguator or attribute token appears adjacent to an entity in the inputs (for example a multiword service name, an explicit page title, a year, or an attribute marker like a birth/producer/service/series cue), include that disambiguator token together with the associated entity in the query rather than dropping or truncating it.
- If `summary_1` contains an explicit page-title token sequence (for example an instruction-like ExactPageTitle or Fetch page title), prefer that exact page-title token sequence as the query value.
- When the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.

delta_p.trigger: If summary_1 contains an "Ambiguities:" or "Bridge / missing fact:" note indicating the target actress identity or her debut film is missing
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the original output contract: produce exactly one short keyword-style field named `query`; remain terse (preferably <=12 words), no sentence grammar or punctuation, use only tokens already visible in the inputs, and allow at most one neutral connector token.
delta_p.change_rule: When triggered, bias the query composition to include any candidate Kaplan-directed film title tokens and any candidate actress name tokens that already appear in the visible inputs (summary_1, retrieved titles/snippets, or question). Combine the distinct visible entity tokens into a single terse query and insert at most one connector token from the question (e.g., "film debut" or "cast") between them to target the missing relation.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not invent or add new entity names or facts; do not produce a full sentence or explanatory text; do not include long quoted spans or the eventual answer; avoid punctuation and multiple connector tokens.
delta_p.output_contract: Produce exactly one field `query` whose value is a single short keyword-style search string (no punctuation, no question marks, no verbs forming assertions), using only tokens visible in the inputs and at most one connector token. Do not output anything else.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The source query omitted candidate actress and specific Kaplan film tokens needed to recover the bridge. This small prompt tweak instructs the agent to prefer any visible film-title and actress-name tokens and to join them with the 'film debut' relation, increasing chance of retrieving the missing debut evidence without changing the module's interface or producing answers.

delta_p.trigger: summary_1 shows multiple Kaplan film candidates or no English actress mentioned
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep original interface and constraints: produce exactly one field named `query`, a single terse keyword-style search string (<=12 words), no sentence punctuation, use only tokens visible in `question` and/or `summary_1` except for at most one neutral connector token.
delta_p.change_rule: When triggered, override lexical choice to prioritize a broad filmography-level seed: emit `Jonathan Kaplan` plus one connector token `filmography` as the sole query (i.e., output the single token sequence: Jonathan Kaplan filmography).
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not add specific film titles, actor names, or debut claims that do not already appear in `question` or `summary_1`; avoid full sentences, question marks, or explanatory words; do not use more than one connector token.
delta_p.output_contract: Produce exactly one `query` string: the short keyword query `Jonathan Kaplan filmography`. Must obey the terse formatting rules (single string, no punctuation, <=12 words).
delta_p.why_local_transition: summary_1 is ambiguous about which Kaplan-directed drama and lacks any stated English actress; a filmography-level retrieval is the smallest targeted step likely to recover the missing cast and debut evidence.

visible_agent_inputs.question: This American drama film directed by Jonathan Kaplan starred an English actress who made her film debut in what?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1:
Evidence found:
- "Jonathan Kaplan" — [1]: "Jonathan Kaplan (born November 25, 1947)"
- "The Accused" — [1]: "His film \"The Accused\" (1988)"
- "Immediate Family (film)" — [2]: "Immediate Family is a 1989 drama film directed by Jonathan Kaplan."
- "Glenn Close" — [2]: "It stars Glenn Close and James Woods"
- "James Woods" — [2]: "It stars Glenn Close and James Woods"
- "Project X (1987 film)" — [3]: "Project X is a 1987 American science fiction comedy-drama film"
- "Matthew Broderick" — [3]: "starring Matthew Broderick and Helen Hunt."
- "Helen Hunt" — [3]: "starring Matthew Broderick and Helen Hunt."
- "Swing Shift (film)" — [4]: "Swing Shift is a 1984 American romantic drama war film"
- "Holly Hunter" — [4]: "Holly Hunter can be seen in one of her first movie roles."
- "Love Field (film)" — [5]: "Love Field is a 1992 American independent drama film"
- "Michelle Pfeiffer" — [5]: "starring Michelle Pfeiffer and Dennis Haysbert."
- "Sandra Bullock filmography" — [6]: "made her film debut with a minor role in the 1987 thriller \"Hangmen\"."
- "Madison Wolfe" — [7]: "She made her film debut in the adventure drama \"On the Road\" (2012)"

Ambiguities:
- "This American drama film directed by Jonathan Kaplan" — passages list multiple Kaplan-directed drama films: "Immediate Family (film)" [2] and "Love Field (film)" [5]; which one is meant is unclear.
- "English actress" — no passage identifies any "English actress" in the casts above; Glenn Close, Michelle Pfeiffer, Helen Hunt, etc., are referenced but are not labeled English in these passages.
- No passage links any named actress above to being English or gives an English actress's debut credit.

Bridge / missing fact:
- Missing identity of the "English actress" who starred in the Kaplan-directed American drama film named in the question.
- Missing the film (title) in which that English actress "made her film debut" (the debut relation for that actress is not present).

Retrieval direction:
- Open "Jonathan Kaplan filmography" or "Jonathan Kaplan" (full filmography section) to list all Kaplan-directed films and principal cast.
- Open "Immediate Family (film) — Wikipedia" (cast section) to verify full cast and any English actresses.
- Open "Love Field (film) — Wikipedia" (cast section) to verify full cast and any English actresses.
- Search query: "Jonathan Kaplan English actress starred" OR "site:wikipedia.org \"Jonathan Kaplan\" \"starred\" \"English\""
Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets above.

current_output.query: Jonathan Kaplan filmography Immediate Family Love Field cast

input_dict.output_requirement: Return exactly {"query": "<one compact second-hop BM25 query>"}. Do not return explanations or multiple queries.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are executing one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Use the unchanged base instruction p together with the complete ORDERED
list of local prompt deltas. The deltas are supplemental behavior guidance
for this sample; they are not outputs and must not be quoted mechanically.

This is a direct delta-context execution probe:
- Do not rewrite, aggregate, or synthesize a new prompt p'.
- Apply every delta in order to the visible inputs.
- Judge success only by producing the endpoint agent output.
- Do not attempt to reproduce or discuss intermediate states.
- Do not infer hidden information from the existence of the deltas.
- Use only the visible agent inputs as factual evidence.
- Never output gold/oracle labels, evaluation metrics, or hidden support.
- Preserve the agent's normal input/output contract.

Agent:
query

Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.
